page: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It features an Intel Xeon 1270 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 480 GB of local SSD storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the average tier for most compute tasks, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1728.79 and a multi-core score of 8815.11. Single-threaded gzip compression performs strongly at 30.56 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is weak, measuring 31.11 GB/sec for read and 14.73 GB/sec for write operations. Passmark testing yields a top-tier memory score of 3192.31, while database operations and Redis SET performance remain in the average tier.

As a bare metal instance, this server offers dedicated resource allocation without hypervisor overhead, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring consistent, non-shared CPU execution. However, the lack of AVX-512 instruction support and weak performance in physics simulations and medium-sized LLM inference represent clear tradeoffs. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, lightweight database operations, and single-threaded data compression tasks.
description: The Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is an x86_64 bare metal server from the Bare Metal SSD family, powered by an Intel Xeon 1270 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at 3.8 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM and 480 GB of local SSD storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core and multi-core compute capabilities, with a strong showing in single-threaded gzip compression and a top-tier Passmark memory score. However, uncached memory bandwidth is weak, and the system lacks AVX-512 support. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated, non-virtualized workloads, making it suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is an x86_64 bare metal server featuring an 8-core Intel Xeon 1270 CPU, 32 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local SSD storage, offering dedicated resources for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server featuring eight physical cores, local SSD storage, and balanced memory performance for consistent general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 1270 processor with 8 physical cores at 3.8 GHz
32 GB of system memory and 480 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier Passmark memory performance and strong single-threaded compression
No virtualization overhead due to bare metal architecture
Best suited for static web serving, moderate database operations, and general-purpose tasks
